explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while vitamins are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 75 from a base nutrition score of 71. Context lifted it by 4 points.

topPros.title: polyphenol reputation is a real plus
topPros.explanation: It brings a widely recognised antioxidant angle beyond the visible macro profile.

topPros.title: contains useful polyphenols
topPros.explanation: Adds extra value beyond the core fat chemistry alone.

topPros.title: very simple ingredient identity
topPros.explanation: A clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.

topCons.title: extremely energy dense
topCons.explanation: Still pure fat, so calories accumulate fast.

topCons.title: quality varies a lot
topCons.explanation: Source and authenticity matter in practice.

topCons.title: easy to overpour without noticing
topCons.explanation: A healthy image can make portion creep happen fast in practice.

closing.summary: Extra Virgin Olive Oil has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
